verdict_reasoning: ATP5F1B (ATP synthase beta subunit, ATP5B) is canonically a mitochondrial inner-membrane F1 catalytic subunit with no conventional TM domain or signal peptide for the secretory pathway. However, extensive published evidence documents ectopic surface translocation of the intact ATP synthase complex—including the beta subunit—to the outer leaflet of the plasma membrane in cancer cells (NSCLC, prostate, leukemia), hepatocytes, endothelial cells, adipocytes, and keratinocytes. The complex is delivered via mitochondrial membrane–plasma membrane fusion (DRP1/KIF5B-dependent), anchoring it stably at the cell surface with the F1 portion (containing beta) directed extracellularly. It functions as a receptor for apoA-I (HDL), angiostatin, and other ligands. This surface pool is cell-type- and state-dependent, qualifying as contextual rather than constitutive baseline surface expression.
